name: Louisiana Roast Beef
recipeInstructions:
1. In a small bowl, combine the chopped onions, celery, bell peppers, butter, and seasonings (salt, white pepper, black pepper, minced garlic, dry mustard, and ground cayenne). Mix well.
2. Place the roast in a large roasting pan with the fat side up.
3. Using a sharp knife, make 6 to 12 deep slits in the meat down to about half an inch from the bottom. Do not cut all the way through.
4. Fill each slit with the vegetable mixture, reserving about one tablespoon of vegetables to rub over the top of the roast.
5. Bake uncovered at 300°F until a meat thermometer reads 160°F for medium doneness (about 3 hours). For a rarer roast, cook until the thermometer reads 140°F.
6. Serve immediately, topped with some pan drippings if desired.
